plan_metadata.name: group  login azure and m365 account with copilot license

steps.description: Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type 'View: Show Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select "View: Show Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" from the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while viewing the "Overview of the Declarative Agent template" in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Type 'Microsoft 365 Agents: Accounts' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Toolkit: Accounts" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click the "Sign in to Azure" dropdown option in the Visual Studio Code command palette to initiate Microsoft 365 authentication.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft authentication popup in Visual Studio Code to proceed with Azure authorization for the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit.
Click the "Allow" button in the Microsoft 365 sign-in prompt to authorize the extension "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" within Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field in the Microsoft sign-in form on the login.microsoftonline.com page to focus the text box for entering credentials.
Enter ${{env:AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}} into the email or username input field on the Microsoft Sign in page at login.microsoftonline.com in a Chrome browser.
Click the "Next" button on the Microsoft Sign-in page to proceed after entering the account email.
Type ${{secret:AZURE_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the 'Password' field on the Microsoft login page for user ${{env:AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}.
Press Enter in the password field on the Microsoft login page to submit credentials for the account ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}.
Click the red close (X) button in the top-right corner of the Google Chrome window to close the Visual Studio Code sign-in confirmation page.
@assertion there's ${{env:AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}} in the "ACCOUNTS" section
Click the "Sign in to Microsoft 365" link in the ACCOUNTS section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel within Visual Studio Code.
Press F1 to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while viewing the "Overview of the Declarative Agent template" in the README.md preview.
Type 'Microsoft 365 Agents: Accounts' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field to search for Microsoft 365 Agents-related commands.
Press Enter to select "Microsoft 365 Agents: Accounts" from the VS Code command palette dropdown.
Click the "Sign in to Microsoft 365" option in the account dropdown menu at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface to initiate Microsoft 365 authentication.
Press Enter to activate the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 Toolkit account permission prompt bar at the top of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Use another account" option on the Microsoft sign-in page within Google Chrome to initiate logging in with a different account.
Enter  ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME_EnableCopilotAccess}} into the 'Email or phone' input field on the Microsoft login page in Google Chrome.
@assertion you can see the ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME_EnableCopilotAccess}} in the input field.
Click the "Next" button on the Microsoft sign-in page in Google Chrome after entering the account email address.

Type  ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD_EnableCopilotAccess}} into the password input field on the Microsoft account login page for user ${{M365:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME_EnableCopilotAccess}} in Google Chrome.
Click the blue "Sign in" button on the Microsoft account password entry screen in Google Chrome.
Click the "Close" (X) button in the top-right corner of the Google Chrome window displaying a Visual Studio Code sign-in confirmation.
@assertion there's ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME_EnableCopilotAccess}} in the "ACCOUNTS" section
